title: Build a correlation matrix with any psych::fa(cor=...) method
summary: Compute a variable × variable correlation (or covariance) matrix and write it as both a CSV and a three-sheet .xlsx workbook. Intended as the matrix feeding a downstream `fa` run, or as a standalone diagnostic.
description: Loads the chosen subset of columns, optionally consulting `meta` to resolve per-column types for mixed/biserial/polyserial dispatch, and computes pairwise correlations using the selected method. Writes two artifacts: a plain CSV (blank leading header cell, variable names in the first column) and a three-sheet xlsx with a `correlation` sheet (R), an `n_obs` sheet (pairwise sample sizes), and a `meta` sheet (method + variable count).
usage:
[["factor-analysis::correlation"]]
input = "./data.csv"
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "cor1"
method = "pearson"
# optional:
meta = "./meta.csv"
vars = ["q1", "q2", "q3"]

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: Path to the input file. CSV or SPSS `.sav`/`.zsav`.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Base directory for outputs. Files land in `<run_dir>/ocinfo/<run_name>_factor_analysis/`.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run; no `..`, `/`, or `\`.

arguments.name: method
arguments.description: Correlation method. See the Correlation methods note for the full list.

arguments.name: meta
arguments.description: Optional variable metadata CSV. Required for `mixed` / `biserial` / `polyserial` to determine per-column types.

arguments.name: vars
arguments.description: Subset of input columns to include. Empty = all columns.

arguments.name: correct
arguments.description: Continuity correction for zero cells in contingency tables used by tetrachoric/polychoric/yule. Matches psych defaults.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether CSV inputs have a header row. Ignored for `.sav` inputs.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Worker thread count for per-pair correlation parallelism. `1` runs serially. Results are deterministic regardless of thread count.

examples.title: Output: <run_name>.csv
examples.body: Plain CSV. First column is variable names (header cell blank); remaining columns are the upper+lower triangle of R in variable order.

examples.title: Output: <run_name>.xlsx
examples.body:
Three sheets:
  correlation — R with bold headers and 0.0000 numeric format
  n_obs       — pairwise sample sizes matching the correlation layout
  meta        — method and variable count

notes: Correlation methods: pearson, covariance, tetrachoric, polychoric, mixed, biserial, polyserial, yuleb, yuleq, yuley. All methods are implemented (Phases 1–2), matching psych::fa(cor=...) conventions.
The `covariance` method produces the unnormalised sample covariance (divisor n-1), not a correlation.
Pairwise sample sizes on the `n_obs` sheet let downstream consumers weight the matrix appropriately.

Type vs. method compatibility: tetrachoric requires dichotomous columns; polychoric requires ordinal (or dichotomous); biserial/polyserial pair numeric with the categorical partner. If meta is omitted the default is numeric, which will fail for the categorical methods.
